item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Negative crit I guess
– hide block

I found the inclusion of the bunny's dialog jarring and unclinical. I'm also just not interested in the narrative itself; am I supposed to feel sad the bunny doesn't like answering questions?

It's ultimately just an article that I read with not much to remember it by. 🤷‍♀️

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Standard upvote from me is I read it without skimming = +1
in addition to that I do actually like the article. But I have to give it a -1 and here's why.

Some of the tone just isn't properly clinical of what an SCP document would contain:

they were later found to have a substance resembling chocolate in their stomach.

Is an example of it. The substance would be defined, or undefined. If it was chocolate, it would say " have chocolate in their stomach". If it was some other thing, you might say "have a substance identified as XXXX in their stomach". If it was some unknown thing, you might say "have an unidentifiable substance in their stomach".

This is not the only tone problem in the article but it is the one that broke me and made me nope. Again, I do like your thing that does horrible thing, but the writing needs work.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article was non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as the author sourced the image has coming from here. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The skip is appropriately contained and incapable of causing harm by itself. It also appears to be pretty consistent in its behavior - though the interview logs imply something could change if provoked.

So right now it should stay where it is. It won't be a danger to anyone that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not bump up this scp to Euclid or keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems like a backstory for a bunny-like entity from the unnamed anomalous land

“you took it from me” as a response to “what is your name” is clearly a coincidence but I think it should be explored

item.title: Plot Holes?
item.content:encoded:
In the description, it reads that only the subject can hear the voice of SCP-4033-1, but yet there are interview logs as if it was somehow recorded.

Also, when someone other than the subject attempts to participate in the hunt, why would the "Easter Bunny" attempt to say anything to them at all if they wouldn't be able to hear it? Does 4033-1 speak aloud? Like a phantom voice? Does SCP 4033-1 know this?

I liked the concept of some sort of paranormal easter-egg hunt, but it seems to have creepy horror tropes for the sake of it. Overall I feel it needs some polish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't help but read all of SCP 4033s text in a Barney voice. Now all I hear is evil Barney. All jokes aside, the idea seemed a little generic at first glance. However, the implication that the anomaly has been permanently altered by the interviews to the point no further testing is allowed peaked my interest. No upvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrible way to go by having either, what I'm assuming to be, an egg or a living rabbit.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing the Easter Bunny was upset about being "done to him" was self-awareness, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any doubt in anybody's minds that it was a Cadbury Creme Egg cad-buried in those brains?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, Instead of [EXPUNGED], i'd use a couple of these. █████

Nonetheless, this really creeped me out. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To tell the truth, I kinda figured a bunny appeared in their brain.

oh god

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose it isn't for everyone :-(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really see the appeal honestly. 1-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the advice. I'll definitely change the O5 Command bit, and add another question for the D-class.

I'm not sure I want to take out the expungements, but I agree it's super obvious on what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has potential. Novote for now.

I feel like expunging what appears in the subjects brain doesn't add anything. Expungement is most effective when it leaves the reader wondering/fearing what it's hiding. I bet 99% of readers would aggree that it's chocolate.

SCP-4033-1 does not respond for 4 minutes.

4 minutes seems like a really long time for the D-class to just be standing there in silence. Maybe have them ask one more question?

Also O5 Command making a ruling on a Safe skip seems like overkill. That seems like something a Site Director or Lead Researcher should do.

Still, an Easter Bunny that kills people by putting chocolate in their brains is certainly unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really good for a first scp, 2 things though the phrase "and the ability to speak and die" sounds weird, maybe change it to, subject will still be able to vocalize, but will die within 2 minutes. Also maybe don't use eh as much in the logs, it sounds out of place, and a bit weird.
No vote for now, but I might change it in the future.
EDIT: upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consider it handled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You still have a stray XXXX in the interview, FYI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it but not enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote for now: too much data expunged for my personal taste, although i get what you're going for :P

item.title: Author Babbling on a Bit
item.content:encoded:
Yay! First SCP up on the site!

Major thanks to Lazar Lyusternik, Gummy_Dragon and rattles for critting this. Expect picture on the article soon.

Enjoy!

EDIT: Picture is up.
